title: Stock Market Sectors 101: A Guide to All 11 Sectors
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: The stock market comprises 11 sectors: information technology, financials, communication services, consumer discretionary, industrials, materials, consumer stap
excerpt: The stock market comprises 11 sectors: information technology, financials, communication services, consumer discretionary, industrials, materials, consumer staples, energy, utilities, health care, and real estate. Consumer staples and utilities are often considered defensive sectors.

content.introduction: The stock market comprises 11 sectors: information technology, financials, communication services, consumer discretionary, industrials, materials, consumer staples, energy, utilities, health care, and real estate. Consumer staples and utilities are often considered defensive sectors.
body: Stock Market Sectors 101: A Guide to All 11 Sectors · Information technology. · Financials. · Communication services. · Consumer discretionary.
Stock Market Sectors 101: The 11 Sectors of the Market · Materials · Financials · Consumer discretionary · Communication services · Health care.

# Stock Market Sectors List. The Bullish Bears break down a list of the 11 stock market sectors. Sectors are the umbrella of industries and sub-industries containing the stock symbols and companies that traders and investors buy and sell. ## List of Stock Market Sectors. * List of Stock Market Sectors. * Defensive and Cyclical Stock Market Sectors. ## Stock Market Sectors Basics. There are 11 different sectors in the stock market. How are the different stock sectors doing? ## Defensive and Cyclical Stock Market Sectors. We mentioned that there are 11 sectors in the stock market sectors list. Hence, most of the stock sectors listed are in the cyclical category. The consumer staples sector is a group of stocks that includes companies that produce and sell items consumers use every day, such as food and beverages, housewares, drugs, and tobacco. What Stock Market Sectors Do Well in a Recession? What Is an Example of a Stock Sector?
